tasks.title: Intake temporary visible-slice art
tasks.summary: Import the selected local floor, room-reference, turret, and prop assets with deterministic provenance and Unity-ready derivatives.
tasks.objective: Create the sole accepted local-art intake for the Stage 1 visible slice so every art consumer uses the same preserved sources, checksums, provenance, and import settings.
tasks.context: The assets under C:/Users/Yeeroo/Desktop/sprites are prototype inputs generated for this project. Intake must preserve originals, record provenance and prototype-only status, and reject any source whose origin cannot be recorded.
inputs: C:/Users/Yeeroo/Desktop/sprites/level_idea_1.png when present, plus a deliberately selected small prop subset
implementation_notes: This task requires a local worktree with access to C:/Users/Yeeroo/Desktop/sprites; a GitHub-only web agent cannot complete it.

Use only ordinary non-destructive Git commands in the fresh worktree: status, fetch, switch, add explicit owned paths, commit, and push. Do not use reset --hard, checkout --, or improvised cross-shell file commands.
Add only task-local leaf .meta files. Do not add shared ancestor metadata such as Runtime/Presentation.meta, Tests/EditMode/Presentation.meta, Tests/PlayMode/Presentation.meta, or UI.meta.
Do not create scenes, prefabs, UI, gameplay code, registries, or persistence state.
Rollback: remove the three task-owned roots together; no runtime migration is required.
Milestone-cap spend: 0.25 focused lead day.
acceptance_criteria: Selected originals are preserved without silent regeneration or renaming and every accepted file has source, provenance, prototype-only status, and SHA-256 recorded.
Unity-ready derivatives use explicit import settings appropriate to top-down 2D presentation, including alpha handling where applicable.
The real tile and turret assets are included; the floor is not replaced by a procedural substitute.
No unknown-rights asset, gameplay logic, scene, prefab, UI, registry, or durable state is introduced.
verification: Automated: recompute every recorded SHA-256 and fail on missing, extra, or mismatched inventory entries; verify changed paths stay inside the three owned roots.
Unity: import with Unity 6000.3.19f1 and confirm no import/compiler errors and expected texture dimensions, alpha, filter, wrap, compression, and pixels-per-unit settings.
Manual: inspect the floor, turret, and selected props at intended camera scale and record suitability limitations without claiming final-art acceptance.
proof_required: Exact changed-path list and Unity import log
Contact sheet or inspector captures of accepted assets
Explicit list of rejected or deferred local candidates
edge_cases: JPEG floor input has no alpha channel.
A generated image contains a checkerboard baked into RGB rather than true transparency.
Two local files have similar names or visually duplicate content.
non_goals: Do not create final art or accept visual quality for release.
Do not implement room, turret, HUD, loadout, camera, or scene behavior.
tasks.handoff_notes: VS-002 and VS-003 must consume these accepted assets read-only and may not independently re-import them.
tasks.notes: Authoritative through merged visible-slice amendment PR #104.

tasks.title: Build the dark industrial room presentation shell
tasks.summary: Create a task-local visual room package using the accepted floor and props without taking collision, encounter, or mission authority.
tasks.objective: Turn the debug rectangle into a readable dark industrial room shell with floor, walls, doors, props, markers, sorting, and lighting presentation that can later be composed by VS-007.
tasks.context: This is presentation over accepted EH-004/EH-005 architectural inputs. The package must use VS-001 art and remain detachable from gameplay truth.
inputs: VS-001 accepted art inventory and Unity-ready derivatives
EH-004 Stage1BenchmarkArena and EH-005 Stage1ShortRouteShell as read-only architectural references
implementation_notes: Use the real accepted VS-001 floor asset. A replaceable sprite field is allowed; a procedural generator is not the default floor.
Do not edit any .unity scene or duplicate EH-004/EH-005 collision and geometry authority.
Add only exact leaf-folder metadata; never add shared ancestor .meta files.
When operating as a GitHub web agent, use the authenticated connector only and do not attempt random local git, gh, clone, shell-authentication, or browser-login commands.
Rollback: remove the two owned folders; no gameplay or save migration is required.
Milestone-cap spend: 0.35 focused lead day.
acceptance_criteria: A task-local prefab/package presents a dark tiled floor, bounded walls/doors, selected props, room markers, sorting, and restrained lighting without adding collision or mission truth.
The floor is readable at prototype camera scale and does not overpower actors, projectiles, telegraphs, or HUD.
No scene, gameplay authority, registry, persistence, or WP-010-owned path changes.
verification: Unity 6000.3.19f1 compilation and focused PlayMode tests pass.
Automated component audit proves no Collider2D, Rigidbody2D, camera, mission, encounter, or persistence authority exists in the package.
Manual 1920x1080 capture demonstrates floor continuity, prop readability, and actor contrast.
edge_cases: Floor replacement sprite is absent.
Camera shows a room edge.
Reduced effects disables optional lighting.
non_goals: Do not implement collisions, encounters, objectives, rewards, or final art.
tasks.handoff_notes: VS-007 composes the package; it must not fork its visuals into the integration scene.
tasks.notes: Consumes the accepted VS-001 art package.

tasks.title: Present the accepted Blaster Turret
tasks.summary: Attach the accepted standing-turret art to EN-007 through a detachable, read-only presentation layer.
tasks.objective: Make the Blaster Turret visibly communicate health, damage, warning, firing, recovery, destruction, and restart state without changing enemy or projectile authority.
tasks.context: EN-002, EN-003, and EN-007 remain authoritative. Presentation reads accepted state/events and the VS-001 turret asset only.
implementation_notes: Consume immutable state/events; never apply damage, advance cadence, choose targets, or execute projectiles.
Do not edit Assets/ShooterMover/ContentPackages/Enemies/BlasterTurret/ or any .unity scene.
Warnings must remain understandable without color and under reduced effects.
Add only exact leaf-folder metadata; never add shared ancestor .meta files.
When operating as a GitHub web agent, use the authenticated connector only and do not attempt random local git, gh, clone, shell-authentication, or browser-login commands.
Rollback: remove the two owned folders; EN-007 behavior remains intact.
Milestone-cap spend: 0.30 focused lead day.
acceptance_criteria: The accepted turret sprite tracks authoritative active, warning, firing, recovery, damaged, health, destroyed/deactivated, and restart-generation states.
Warning timing remains EN-007-owned and readable by shape/text/count as well as color.
Presentation removal leaves EN-007 simulation behavior unchanged.
verification: Unity compilation and focused PlayMode tests cover the full warning/fire/recovery/damage/deactivation/restart path.
A parity test proves the presentation never mutates enemy health, cadence, target selection, or projectile execution.
Manual default, reduced-effects, and grayscale captures remain readable.
edge_cases: Damage and warning occur in the same frame.
Restart generation changes while a warning is pending.
Health reaches zero before a scheduled shot.
non_goals: Do not rebalance or rewrite EN-007.
Do not add targeting, projectile, encounter, reward, or persistence authority.
tasks.handoff_notes: VS-007 binds the package to the accepted EN-007 fixture.

tasks.title: Add the general combat HUD
tasks.summary: Provide a temporary but reusable combat HUD around the existing WP-010 four-slot strip.
tasks.objective: Show player health, thruster, reticle, hit confirmation, focused-enemy health, room/objective state, and restart framing so the prototype reads as a game screen.
tasks.context: The HUD is read-only presentation. WP-010 exclusively owns its four-slot weapon strip; this task reserves space for it and must not wrap, duplicate, restyle, or test it.
tasks.owner_role: Mission, Persistence, Rewards, and UI Builder
inputs: EN-002/EN-003 enemy health and Unity bridge
WP-010 layout region as an exclusive read-only input
Reusable room/objective/restart projection intent recorded from superseded PR #107
implementation_notes: Qualify UnityEngine.Application explicitly anywhere the ShooterMover.Application namespace could shadow it.
Use injected read-only status sources. Do not create player-health, objective-completion, mission, save, or enemy mutation authority.
Do not edit WP-010-owned paths or its focused test.
Do not edit any .unity scene. Add only exact leaf-folder metadata and never shared ancestor UI/Test metadata.

When operating as a GitHub web agent, use the authenticated connector only. Do not attempt gh, local Git, cloning, shell authentication checks, or browser login; start a fresh branch from current main through the connector.
Rollback: remove the two owned folders; combat and enemy simulation remain unchanged.
Milestone-cap spend: 0.45 focused lead day.
acceptance_criteria: HUD presents player health, thruster, reticle, confirmed hit, focused-enemy health, room/objective framing, restart hint, and reduced-effects warning from injected read-only state.
Layout reserves the WP-010 strip region and remains readable at 1920x1080 and one smaller 16:9 resolution.
Critical state is readable without color and no gameplay, persistence, objective, or WP-010 authority is introduced.
verification: Unity compilation and focused PlayMode tests cover visibility, safe margins, state projection, restart reset, and reduced-effects behavior.
Tests prove confirmed-hit UI appears only from accepted hit facts and that HUD actions never mutate source state.
Manual keyboard/controller-distance and grayscale captures show no overlap with the WP-010 region.
edge_cases: No focused enemy.
Zero thruster or critical player health.
Objective text is long.
Restart occurs while transient hit feedback is visible.
non_goals: Do not implement final UI art, localization, inventory, rewards, saves, or mission truth.
Do not edit Stage1VisibleSlice.unity or WP-010.
tasks.handoff_notes: VS-007 composes this HUD beside the unmodified WP-010 strip.
tasks.notes: May start immediately; it does not depend on VS-001.

tasks.title: Add the temporary fixed-loadout selector
tasks.summary: Present the accepted WP-008 fixed four-slot comparisons before room entry without inventory or persistence.
tasks.objective: Provide a deterministic keyboard/controller loadout-selection flow that emits one immutable WP-008 fixture for VS-007 and resets cleanly.
tasks.context: This is a temporary selector, not an inventory system. It consumes WP-008 directly and treats WP-010 as entirely outside its ownership.
tasks.owner_role: Mission, Persistence, Rewards, and UI Builder
inputs: Reusable deterministic menu-state/input intent recorded from superseded PR #108
implementation_notes: Consume WP-008 fixture IDs and ordered four slots directly; do not use reflection into WP-010 or Assembly-CSharp.
Use InputSystem.AddDevice<T>() or a locally defined valid helper in tests; do not call the nonexistent bare AddDevice<T>() inherited from InputTestFixture.
Confirmation emits an immutable fixture selection only. It does not load a scene or configure live mounts.
Do not edit any .unity scene. Add only exact leaf-folder metadata and never shared ancestor UI/Test metadata.

When operating as a GitHub web agent, use the authenticated connector only. Do not attempt gh, local Git, cloning, shell authentication checks, or browser login; start a fresh branch from current main through the connector.
Rollback: remove the two owned folders; no saved state or migration exists.
Milestone-cap spend: 0.15 focused lead day.
acceptance_criteria: The selector shows exactly the approved WP-008 fixtures and their ordered four-slot weapon IDs.
Keyboard and controller navigation, wraparound, confirm, cancel, terminal behavior, and deterministic restart reset work.
No inventory, unlock, reward, shop, reroll, random modifier, PlayerPrefs, save, scene loading, reflection bridge, or package tuning is introduced.
verification: Unity compilation and focused PlayMode tests pass with real virtual keyboard/controller devices.
Tests cover wraparound, confirm/cancel terminal states, invalid input neutrality, and ResetForRestart returning the default fixture with no retained confirmation.
Static audit proves direct WP-008 consumption and no WP-010 reflection or persistence API usage.
edge_cases: Simultaneous confirm and navigation input.
Controller disconnect.
Restart after confirmation.
Unknown fixture ID fails closed.
non_goals: Do not implement editable per-slot inventory or actual mount configuration.
Do not edit Stage1VisibleSlice.unity, WP-008 definitions, or WP-010.
tasks.handoff_notes: VS-007 consumes the immutable confirmed fixture and owns room entry/live composition.
tasks.notes: May start immediately; it does not depend on VS-001.

tasks.title: Apply the orthographic camera and readability pass
tasks.summary: Create a task-local orthographic camera/readability rig with bounded framing, safe HUD margins, and reduced-effects parity.
tasks.objective: Provide stable room framing and visual-readability constraints for the visible slice without writing movement, combat, or global settings authority.
tasks.context: The camera consumes accepted movement state and injected reduced-effects state. VS-007 owns scene composition; this task owns only a reusable rig/configuration and focused proof.
inputs: MT-010 movement actor runtime as read-only follow input
Visible-slice safe-margin and reduced-effects rules from the merged amendment
outputs: Reusable orthographic camera/readability rig or configuration
implementation_notes: Do not edit any .unity scene, Rigidbody2D, player transform, movement state, combat simulation, global quality setting, or persistence setting.
Essential warnings and information must survive reduced effects and grayscale; color is supplemental.
Add only exact leaf-folder metadata and never shared ancestor .meta files.

When operating as a GitHub web agent, use the authenticated connector only. Do not attempt gh, local Git, cloning, shell authentication checks, or browser login; start a fresh branch from current main through the connector.
Rollback: remove the two owned folders; accepted movement and project rendering remain unchanged.
Milestone-cap spend: 0.25 focused lead day.
acceptance_criteria: A task-local orthographic rig/configuration provides bounded follow or room clamp, reference framing, HUD safe margins, and screen-edge warning constraints.
Default and reduced-effects modes preserve timing and essential information without depending on color.
The rig does not mutate player movement, combat, global settings, scenes, or persistence.
verification: Unity compilation and focused PlayMode tests cover 16:9 framing, clamp boundaries, safe margins, restart reset, reduced-effects parity, and grayscale warning readability.
Tests prove the camera never writes Rigidbody2D velocity, player position, movement intent, combat state, or global quality state.
Manual captures at 1920x1080 and one smaller 16:9 resolution show stable actor/telegraph framing.
edge_cases: Player reaches every room boundary.
Viewport aspect changes within supported 16:9 sizes.
Restart occurs at a clamp edge.
non_goals: Do not implement cinematic camera effects or final post-processing.
Do not edit Stage1VisibleSlice.unity or global project settings.
tasks.handoff_notes: VS-007 instantiates and binds the rig in the sole integration scene.
tasks.notes: May start immediately; it does not depend on VS-001.

tasks.title: Compose and prove the final Stage 1 visible slice
tasks.summary: Serially compose the accepted movement, combat, enemy, room, HUD, loadout, weapon-strip, and camera packages into one playable prototype scene.
tasks.objective: Deliver one screen-visible Stage 1 prototype where the human lead selects a fixed loadout, enters a readable room, moves, aims, fires, damages and kills an enemy, reads turret warning and HUD feedback, and restarts cleanly.
tasks.context: This is the sole serialized scene owner and final composition boundary. It may use one deterministic TestSupport-only session health value but introduces no durable game state.
inputs: All accepted VS-001 through VS-006 outputs
Accepted movement, four-mount combat, weapon, enemy, encounter, restart, and evidence authorities listed in the merged amendment
implementation_notes: This task starts only after all dependencies merge and is the sole owner of Stage1VisibleSlice.unity.
Compose accepted packages; do not fork their logic or duplicate their serialized assets into new authorities.
One deterministic session-only player-vital value is allowed inside the owned TestSupport composition. It cannot persist or become MissionRunState/campaign health.
No build-settings, registry, save, inventory, reward, unlock, mission persistence, or durable room-clear change.
Add only exact task-local metadata; never rewrite shared ancestor .meta files.
Rollback: remove the four owned outputs; accepted component packages remain independently valid.
Milestone-cap spend: 0.80 focused lead day plus up to 0.60 day explicit integration contingency for compile, composition, readability, or restart defects only.
acceptance_criteria: The player selects a WP-008 loadout, enters the room, moves, aims, fires, receives session-only health feedback, damages and kills at least one enemy, observes the complete EN-007 warning/fire/recovery/deactivation path, sees objective/room-clear presentation, and quick-restarts cleanly.
The room uses accepted floor/prop/turret art, general HUD, unmodified WP-010 strip, loadout selector, and camera rig and reads as a game screen rather than debug geometry.
Fifty restart cycles leave no duplicate scene objects, callbacks, projectiles, enemies, HUD owners, camera owners, selected-loadout state, or session-health state.
No durable state, build-settings edit, MissionRunState, inventory, reward, save, registry, or persistence output is created.
verification: Unity 6000.3.19f1 imports and compiles the merged dependency tree and focused end-to-end PlayMode suite.
Run the fifty-restart leak test and record object/callback/projectile/enemy/HUD/camera counts.
Capture default, reduced-effects, and grayscale evidence plus a manual playable check at reference resolution.
Where available, capture through EH-008/EH-009/EH-010 after the direct focused suite passes.
proof_required: Exact scene ownership and no-durable-state audit
edge_cases: Restart during turret warning or active projectiles.
Loadout confirm/cancel transition.
Enemy destroyed on the same frame as incoming damage.
Reduced effects enabled before scene entry.
non_goals: Do not claim final art, final balance, formal Stage 1 gate acceptance, inventory, rewards, progression, or campaign persistence.
Do not expand into WP-012, EN-012, EN-013, AR, GATE, networking, mobile, analytics, or storefront work.
tasks.handoff_notes: This is the only serial integration task; deeper evidence work resumes after its proof.
tasks.notes: Uses the 0.60-day amendment contingency only for bounded integration defects.
